metadata.methodology: The Adopted Budget is prepared by the Finance Division, reviewed by department heads, and adopted by City Council resolution. Figures represent appropriated amounts, not actuals. Operating expenditures are broken into personnel, maintenance/operations, and capital outlay within each department.

dataFields.description: Sum of all line items within each of the 7 General Fund departments
Individual appropriations (e.g., Sheriff Contract, Fire Contract)
GreenLedger-assigned tags linking budget items to sustainability categories

limitations: Adopted figures may differ from actual expenditures — actuals are available after fiscal year close
Sustainability tags are editorially assigned by GreenLedger, not official city classifications
Line item granularity limited to what the adopted document discloses

metadata.methodology: One-page summary infographic produced by the city's Finance Division. Totals are rounded for readability and match the adopted budget's summary tables.

dataFields.description: Total citywide operating expenditures across all funds

limitations: Rounded figures — use full adopted budget for precise amounts
Summary only — does not break down by department or fund

metadata.updateFrequency: Updated when new budget is adopted; historical data available continuously
metadata.methodology: Interactive portal powered by OpenGov Inc. Pulls from the city's financial system. Allows filtering by fund, department, and fiscal year. Historical data represents actuals for closed years and adopted/amended for current year.
limitations: Adopted year figures may be amended mid-year and differ from final actuals
Department names and structures may change across years
Portal availability depends on city contract with OpenGov

metadata.methodology: Same preparation process as current year. Used for year-over-year comparison in GreenLedger's trend analysis.
limitations: Adopted figures — actual expenditures may have differed
Used only for trend comparison, not standalone analysis

metadata.updateFrequency: Updated periodically by city staff (no fixed schedule)
metadata.methodology: Official city webpage maintained by the Public Works or Sustainability office. Lists active programs with qualitative descriptions. Quantitative metrics (e.g., 350,000 kWh solar) are self-reported by the city.

dataFields.description: Annual kWh from Corporate Yard solar installation
Energy reduction from traffic signal LED conversion

limitations: No fixed update schedule — metrics may lag behind current operations
Quantitative figures are self-reported, not independently audited
Page does not provide time-series data for trend analysis

metadata.methodology: Dedicated program page describing the recycled water distribution system. Key metrics (722M gallons, 25 miles, 200 acres) are self-reported program statistics from program inception in 1988.

dataFields.description: Gallons of potable water conserved per year via recycled water
Acres of parks/medians irrigated with recycled water

limitations: 722M gallon figure may be cumulative program estimate, not independently metered annually
Infrastructure details (cost, capacity) are not publicly disclosed on this page
No update timestamp on the page itself

metadata.methodology: Official U.S. Census Bureau count from the 2020 Decennial Census. Population figure of 51,460 used for all per-capita calculations in GreenLedger.

dataFields.description: Total resident population of Cerritos city

limitations: 2020 count — actual current population may differ
Per-capita calculations assume stable population between census years
ACS estimates available but with larger margins of error

metadata.methodology: Adopted by City Council as a guiding policy document. Goals and objectives were developed through council workshops and community input. GreenLedger maps its features to specific goals editorially.

dataFields.description: Editorially assigned mapping of app features to strategic goals

limitations: Alignment mappings are editorial interpretations, not official city endorsements
Strategic plan objectives are aspirational — progress metrics not published
